question.text: What determines whether a currency option is classified as European or American in India?

answer.text: In India, all exchange-traded currency options are European-style — this is mandated by exchange/SEBI rules and cannot be chosen by individual traders
The trader can choose European or American style when placing the order
USDINR options are American-style; cross-currency options are European
The exchange determines the style randomly for each contract

explanation.text: **The Logic:** All exchange-traded options in India (including currency) are European by exchange rules. This is a design choice — European options are simpler to price, don't require early assignment processing, and Black-Scholes/Garman-Kohlhagen directly applies. **The Trap:** Traders have no choice — the exchange determines the exercise style for all contracts. All Indian exchange-traded currency options are European regardless of the currency pair. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series I Ch4 confirms European style for currency options. This aligns with equity options (VIII) and commodity options (XVI) — all European in India. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** European-style means your option can only be exercised at expiry — even if it's deep ITM during the contract life, you can't exercise early. You can always SELL the option to capture its value.
